commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Israel, when you were judges in Assyria, ver. 5. (Haydock)
Conversed. No one could be alone with women, in the East, without suspicion. Ver. 61. Neighbour; stoning or strangling them, unless they gave them up to Nabuchodonosor's officers, ver. 5. (Calmet) Ver. 64. Forward. By this first prophetical act Daniel acquired fame, (Worthington) which he retained till the death of Astyages. (Maldonatus; Menochius) Ver. 65. Astyages, or Darius, chap. v. 31. This belongs to the following chapter (Calmet) or to the 9th. (Worthington)
Cyrus. Little is known about his birth or death. Yet all agree that he conquered the Chaldeans. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
